menu_title: Red Flag -- Raising a Risk Before It Explodes
menu_desc: Raise a risk out loud: likelihood, impact, mitigation, decision
title_sub: Raising a Risk Before It Becomes a Disaster
ch3_sub: The risk is real. The room wants to move on.
ch5_sub: She will call you dramatic. Say it anyway.
ch6_sub: Quick fire, three role-plays, and a room that does not want the truth

plan_row.tema: Red Flag -- Raising a Risk Before It Explodes
plan_row.foco: Probability (may / might / could / it is likely that) + mitigation (unless / to mitigate)
plan_row.atividade: Risk card + role-play with a stakeholder who downplays the risk
plan_row.homework: Record a 2-min risk warning: likelihood, impact, mitigation, decision

heading: The Risk Nobody
Flagged
warmup.intro: Last class you asked Marina for exactly one thing:

callback.text: I need you to flag the risk on day three.
callback.speak: I need you to flag the risk on day three.

warmup.box_title: Now it is your turn to flag one -- to people who do not want to hear it.
warmup.box_text: The client's security team has not approved the new database. Nobody is worried. The go-live is in five weeks.

goal.d: Three role-plays, and I say you are dramatic

heading: The Language of the Red
Flag
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to warn people before it is too late

vocab.def: A small signal that something bad may come
vocab.def_pc: a small signal that something bad may come
vocab.hint: A small signal of something bad
vocab.ex: The first warning sign was the silence from their security team.
It is likely that the approval will take four weeks.

vocab.def: Something you need from someone else before you can move
vocab.def_pc: something you need from someone else before you can move
vocab.hint: You cannot move until someone else moves
vocab.ex: The approval is a dependency, and it is not in my control.

vocab.def: To make a risk smaller, before it happens
vocab.def_pc: to make a risk smaller, before it happens
vocab.hint: To make the risk smaller, in advance
vocab.ex: To mitigate it, we can start the tests on the old database.

vocab.def: A plan B, ready before you need it
vocab.def_pc: a plan B, ready before you need it
vocab.hint: Your plan B, ready in advance
vocab.ex: The contingency is to go live with the old database.

vocab.def: The worst thing that can realistically happen
vocab.def_pc: the worst thing that can realistically happen
vocab.hint: The worst thing that can happen
vocab.ex: Worst case, the go-live moves to August.

vocab.def: To make a problem sound smaller than it is
vocab.def_pc: to make a problem sound smaller than it is
vocab.hint: To make it sound smaller than it is
vocab.ex: Do not downplay it just because nobody is worried.

vocab.def: To raise a problem, early and out loud
vocab.def_pc: to raise a problem, early and out loud
vocab.hint: To raise it, early and out loud
vocab.ex: I want to flag a risk before we plan the sprint.

heading: A Risk You Saw
Coming
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a problem at Oracle that everybody saw coming, and nobody flagged.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: what was the warning sign, who could have stopped it, and why did nobody speak?

heading: The Room Is Too
Calm
listenings.text: Diogo, before the steering meeting. I know you want to talk about the database approval. Let me tell you how this room works. If you say the word problem, everybody stops listening and starts defending themselves. So do not bring me a problem. Bring me a risk, with three numbers: how likely it is, what it costs us, and what you want to do about it. And bring a decision. If you just say the client is slow, I will say what everybody says: let us wait and see. Give me something to decide, and give me a date. If you do not, in five weeks this is your fault, not theirs.
comp: What happens in the room if Diogo says the word problem?
Everybody stops listening and starts defending themselves.
What three things does Sophia want with the risk?
How likely it is, what it costs, and what he wants to do about it.
What does she say will happen if he does not bring a decision?
In five weeks it will be his fault, not the client's.

heading: The Warning,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk. The client's security team has not approved the new database, and they have been silent for eleven days. That is the warning sign. It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks, because that is what it took last year. The impact: the migration cannot start, and the go-live moves. Worst case, we lose the whole of July. This is a dependency, and it is not in my control -- but I can mitigate it. My plan is simple. This week, we start the tests on the old database, so we do not lose time. And I need one decision from you today: do we go live with the old database in June, or do we wait for the approval and move the date? Both are acceptable. Doing nothing is not.
comp: The client's security team has been silent for eleven days.
How does he mitigate the risk?
By starting the tests on the old database this week.
What decision does he ask for?
Go live with the old database in June, or wait for the approval and move the date.

heading: The Risk
Card
artefact.title: Database Approval -- Client Security
artefact.reason: Risk: the new database is not approved, and the team has been silent for 11 days
rows: Likelihood: high -- last year the same approval took four weeks
Impact: the migration cannot start; worst case, we lose July
Mitigation: start the tests on the old database this week
Decision needed by Friday: go live on the old database, or move the date
artefact.footer: One risk. One page. One decision. Silence is also a decision -- the expensive one.

grammar.prompt: Which one warns? Which one measures? Which one gives the way out?

discovery.html: "There is a risk that the approval takes four more weeks."
discovery.speak: There is a risk that the approval takes four more weeks.

discovery.html: "It is likely that they answer only in July."
discovery.speak: It is likely that they answer only in July.

discovery.html: "The go-live might move to August."
discovery.speak: The go-live might move to August.

discovery.html: "To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database."
discovery.speak: To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database.

rows: There is a risk that the approval takes four more weeks.
To mitigate it, we + verb / Unless we..., we will...
To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database.
grammar.note: A risk is not a complaint. A complaint has no numbers and no plan, and the room will always answer "let us wait and see". A risk has a likelihood, an impact, a mitigation and a decision -- and a decision cannot be ignored.
mistakes: It has a risk that the vendor delay the tests.
There is a risk that the vendor will delay the tests.
Maybe the server will down in the migration.
The server might go down during the migration.
grammar.mistake_note: Use There is a risk that -- never "it has a risk". And after might / may / could comes the verb with no "to" and no "will": might go down.

heading: How to Raise a
Risk
bank.sub: The warning sign → how likely → the impact → the mitigation → the decision
bank.label: The five lines of a red flag
items: Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk.
The client's security team has been silent for eleven days.
It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks.
Worst case, the go-live moves to August.
To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database this week.
I need one decision from you by Friday.

scenarios: Two developers leave the project in June. Nobody has replaced them, and nobody is worried.
The vendor's test environment has failed twice this month. The go-live depends on it.
The client wants a new feature in the last sprint. Everyone says it is small.
scenarios_sub: Sixty seconds each: the warning sign, how likely, the impact, the plan, the decision.

heading: Do Not Be
Dramatic

lines.html: Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk. The security team has been silent for eleven days.
lines.speak: Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk. The security team has been silent for eleven days.

lines.html: They are always slow. Do not be dramatic.
lines.speak: They are always slow. Do not be dramatic.

lines.html: I am not. It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks -- that is what it took last year.
lines.speak: I am not. It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks. That is what it took last year.

lines.html: The migration cannot start. Worst case, we lose July. It is a dependency, and it is not in my control.
lines.speak: The migration cannot start. Worst case, we lose July. It is a dependency, and it is not in my control.

lines.html: So you are telling me we are going to fail.
lines.speak: So you are telling me we are going to fail.

lines.html: No. To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database this week. The contingency is to go live on the old one in June.
lines.speak: No. To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database this week. The contingency is to go live on the old one in June.

lines.html: Nobody else raised this. Why you?
lines.speak: Nobody else raised this. Why you?

lines.html: Because I saw the warning sign, and I will not downplay it. I need one decision from you by Friday.
lines.speak: Because I saw the warning sign, and I will not downplay it. I need one decision from you by Friday.

comp: How does Sophia react to the first warning?
She says the security team is always slow, and tells him not to be dramatic.
What does she think Diogo is saying?
That the project is going to fail.
What does Diogo ask for at the end?

quickfire.situation: Open the meeting with the risk, in one sentence.
tips: Say it before the agenda, not after.

quickfire.situation: Give the warning sign -- a fact, with a number.
tips: Days of silence, failures, people who left.

quickfire.situation: Say how likely it is, and why.
tips: 'It is likely that..., because last year...'

quickfire.situation: Your director says: 'You are being dramatic.' Answer.
tips: Do not defend your feelings -- repeat the number.
'Eleven days of silence, four weeks last year.'

quickfire.situation: Give the mitigation, this week -- not in July.
tips: Something you can start on Monday.

quickfire.situation: Ask for the decision, with a deadline.

items: If this is so serious, why is nobody else worried?
Are you covering yourself, or are you protecting the project?
What do you want me to tell the client -- that we are not ready?
pressure.followup: Answer first, number second, plan third. Never say "problem" -- say risk, likelihood, and decision.

heading: Raise the
Flag
guided.prompt: Steering meeting. Two minutes. Raise the database risk.
guided.sub: Use the risk card. Five moves, in this order:
chips: I want to flag one risk

heading: Nobody Is
Worried
semi.quote: Everybody says it is fine. Why are you the only one panicking?
semi.sub: She downplays it twice: first she says the team is always slow, then she says you are covering yourself. Do not get emotional. Repeat the numbers, give the mitigation, and ask for a decision with a date.
chips: I am not panicking, I am flagging
eleven days, four weeks last year
I need a decision by Friday

heading: Your Real
Red Flag
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. One risk.
free.sub: Take a real risk on your project at Oracle, today. Raise it out loud: the warning sign, how likely, the impact, the mitigation, and the decision you need -- with a date.

survival.en: I want to flag one risk before we start.
survival.pt: Quero sinalizar um risco antes de começarmos.

survival.en: It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks.
survival.pt: É provável que a aprovação leve mais quatro semanas.

survival.en: Worst case, the go-live moves to August.
survival.pt: No pior cenário, o go-live vai para agosto.

survival.en: To mitigate it, we start the tests this week.
survival.pt: Para mitigar, começamos os testes esta semana.

survival.en: I need one decision from you by Friday.
survival.pt: Preciso de uma decisão sua até sexta.

checks: I can flag a risk before it becomes a problem.
I say how likely it is -- and why.
I can describe the worst case without drama.
I always bring a mitigation I can start this week.
I ask for a decision, with a date.

closing.line: You raised the flag, the room called you dramatic, and you still got the decision.
closing.next_title: Day 14 -- Explain It Like I Am the CFO
closing.next_desc: Defending a technical decision to people who do not speak technology

order_audio.text: Diogo opens the meeting and flags one risk: the security team has been silent for eleven days. Sophia tells him not to be dramatic. He says how likely it is: four more weeks, like last year. He gives the worst case, and the mitigation: start the tests on the old database. Finally, he asks for one decision by Friday.
items: He says how likely it is: four more weeks, like last year.
Diogo flags a risk: eleven days of silence from the security team.
He asks for one decision by Friday.
Sophia tells him not to be dramatic.
He gives the worst case, and the mitigation.

preclass.desc: Raise a risk in front of people who do not want to hear it: warning sign, likelihood, impact, mitigation, decision.
preclass.context: Eleven days of silence from the client's security team. That is the warning sign, and Diogo will not downplay it. In the meeting he does not say "we have a problem" -- he says: "I want to flag one risk." Then he measures it: "It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks, because that is what it took last year." The approval is a dependency: he cannot control it, and the migration cannot start without it. Worst case, the go-live might move to August. Sophia says he is being dramatic. He does not argue about that. He goes to the plan: "To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database this week." The contingency is to go live on the old database in June. And he ends with the only sentence that forces a room to move: "I need one decision from you by Friday."
quiz_ctx: Why does Diogo say "I want to flag one risk" instead of "we have a problem"?
Because a risk has a likelihood, a plan and a decision -- a problem only invites excuses.
Because he is not sure it is real.
Because Sophia does not speak English well.
The go-live might move to August.
The go-live might to move to August.
The go-live might will move to August.
What does he ask for at the end?
preclass.tip_sub: Avisar sem virar o pessimista da sala: probabilidade, impacto, mitigação, decisão. / Warning people without becoming the pessimist in the room.
tip_rows: There is a risk that the approval takes four more weeks.
To mitigate it, we + verbo / Unless we..., we will...
To mitigate it, we start the tests this week.
preclass.tip_key: Depois de might / may / could vem o verbo puro: "might go down" -- nunca "might to go" nem "might will go". E use There is a risk that, jamais "it has a risk".

blanks.hint: Hint: to raise a problem early and out loud / sinalizar
blanks.phrase: I want to flag one risk before we start.
It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks.
blanks.post: that the approval takes four more weeks."

blanks.hint: Hint: the worst thing that can happen / pior cenário
blanks.phrase: Worst case, the go-live moves to August.

blanks.hint: Hint: to make the risk smaller, in advance / mitigar
blanks.phrase: To mitigate it, we start the tests this week.
blanks.post: it, we start the tests this week."

blanks.hint: Hint: you cannot move until someone else moves / dependência
blanks.phrase: The approval is a dependency, and it is not in my control.
blanks.post: , and it is not in my control."

blanks.hint: Hint: may / could -- possibility, no "to" after it
blanks.phrase: The server might go down during the migration.

preclass.order_title: Put the Red Flag in Order
preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of a red flag. Record each one. Steady voice, no drama -- the numbers do the work.
quiz_sit: You see a real risk two months before the go-live. You:
Flag it in the next meeting, with likelihood, impact and a mitigation.
Wait: maybe it will solve itself.
Send a long email to everyone, with no plan.
Your director says: "You are being dramatic." You answer:
"Eleven days of silence, and four weeks last year. That is the number, not a feeling."
The strongest way to end a risk warning is:
"I need one decision from you by Friday."
"Let us wait and see what happens."
think: Take a real risk on your project at Oracle, today. Speak for two minutes: (1) the warning sign, with a number, (2) how likely it is, and why, (3) the impact and the worst case, (4) the mitigation you can start this week, (5) the decision you need, with a date. Record it.
Now the room answers: "Nobody else is worried. Why are you?" Answer out loud. Do not defend your feelings -- repeat the numbers and ask for the decision again. Record it.

complementary.title: Chernobyl (2019)
complementary.desc: Engineers see a serious risk very early. Watch how they report it, and what happens when leaders do not listen.
complementary.tip: After episode 1, write one sentence that clearly names a risk on your own project.

complementary.title: BEP 307 -- Project Management 2: Kickoff Meeting
complementary.desc: A project manager brings up possible obstacles with the client: things that can hit the timeline or the budget.
complementary.tip: Listen for how he raises a problem without sounding negative, and copy two of those sentences.

complementary.title: How to Write Project Risks That Get Attention and Support
complementary.desc: A simple way to describe a risk so that people take it seriously and help you.
complementary.tip: Use the video format to write one risk from your Oracle project in English.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'Last class you gave feedback to one person. Today you tell a whole room something they do not want to hear.' Avise: hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 12. Depois a virada: 'You asked Marina to flag risks. Today you have to do it yourself -- upwards.' Faca a pergunta do box e cale a boca 90 segundos.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, one move, and then you warn me three times -- and I tell you that you are exaggerating.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' Ferramenta, nao aula de vocabulario.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'likely': 'Likely = certain? (No -- probable.)' CCQ 'dependency': 'Can you control a dependency? (No -- someone else does.)' CCQ 'mitigate': 'Do you mitigate before or after the disaster? (Before.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'contingency': 'Is a contingency plan A or plan B? (Plan B, ready in advance.)' CCQ 'downplay': 'If I downplay a risk, do I make it bigger or smaller? (Smaller -- and that is dangerous.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre um problema que todo mundo viu chegar e ninguem sinalizou. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta.
teacher.ch3: Transicao (1 min): 'The room is calm. That is exactly the problem.' Crie o desconforto.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia tells you how the room works -- and what will happen if you say the word problem.' Toque uma vez, sem texto.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'Why is a risk easier to hear than a problem?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read the risk card, then raise the flag out loud, in five moves.' Ele fala; voce marca o que faltou. E o roteiro do role-play guiado.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one warns? Which one measures? Which one gives the way out?' Guie: There is a risk that = nomear; likely/might = medir; To mitigate it = plano.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Nomear, medir, mitigar. Exija 2 frases dele com 'It is likely that...' sobre o projeto real. CCQ: 'Might GO or might TO GO? (might go.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): 'It has a risk' e 'might will'. Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes.
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para um risco REAL dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'She will call you dramatic. Say it anyway.' Suba a tensao.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a Sophia e voce MINIMIZA: 'they are always slow', 'do not be dramatic', 'nobody else raised this'. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a SOPHIA. Depois: 'Why does she resist? What is she afraid of?' Deixe ele falar.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the red flag, done well -- 60 seconds.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What is the mitigation? What is the decision?' -- ele fala.
teacher.scenarios: Scenarios falados (5 min): Tres situacoes, 60 segundos cada, sem preparo: sinal, probabilidade, impacto, mitigacao, decisao. Nao corrija no meio.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here you raise the flag, and I say you are exaggerating. Three times.'
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e a Sophia. Ele levanta o risco usando os cinco movimentos do risk card. Se ele nao pedir DECISAO com data, mande refazer.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Voce minimiza duas vezes: 'everybody says it is fine' e depois 'you are covering yourself'. Ele nao pode se emocionar: repete os numeros, da a mitigacao e pede a decisao com data.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real risk on your project. Two minutes. One decision. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim. So entao 2 correcoes.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas duras, uma a uma, sem aviso. Numero primeiro, plano depois. Se hesitar mais de 3 segundos, repita a pergunta.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these, nobody can say you did not warn them.' Toque cada uma; ele repete com tom calmo -- drama derruba o argumento.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos levantando um risco real com decisao e data.
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos com um risco real (probabilidade + impacto + mitigacao + decisao) e enviar. Anuncie a Aula 14: 'Next time you explain a technical decision to people who do not speak technology -- and who control your budget.'
